title: Desktop MCP Server Management UI (Phase 2 Core Parity) — Revised
consumer_purpose: Desktop users can add, edit, remove, configure, and authenticate MCP servers (both local and remote) with proper persistence across restarts
claim_atoms: Dialog supports adding both local (command-based) and remote (URL-based) MCP servers with all config fields
Dialog supports editing existing MCP server configuration (name, URL/command, environment, headers, timeout, OAuth)
Dialog supports removing MCP servers with confirmation dialog; removes config + disconnects + removes auth tokens
MCP config changes persist across app restarts via electron-store (following Phase 1 custom agents pattern)
Pre-existing MCP servers from electron-store are re-created in the MCP service on startup (sync on mount)
OAuth authentication can be triggered from the UI; credentials can be removed
Server list shows health status indicators, enables/disables toggle, and supports search/filter
Follows the same UI pattern as dialog-edit-agent.tsx and custom agents IPC flow
content:
# Desktop MCP Server Management UI — Revised Implementation Plan

## Review Corrections Applied
This revision addresses all 3 blocking findings from the critic review:

1. **ipc.ts state verified** — `RESERVED_STORE_NAMES` and validation helpers exist at **module level** (lines 10, 22-35), not inside `registerIpcHandlers`. The plan now references the actual current state.
2. **SDK API surface verified** — All 5 methods exist: `sdk.client.mcp.add()`, `.connect()`, `.disconnect()`, `.auth.remove()`, `.auth.authenticate()`. Verified from `packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/sdk.gen.ts` (lines 2098-2233 for Mcp class, 1959-2097 for Auth2 class). Config types `McpLocalConfig`, `McpRemoteConfig`, `McpOAuthConfig` confirmed in `types.gen.ts` (lines 1131-1175).
3. **dialog-select-mcp.tsx acknowledged as destructive edits** — The file is 81% dirty (261 uncommitted lines out of 323). The proposed changes replace ~40% of the component logic including the inline add form, handleRemove, DropdownMenu items, and button bar.
4. **Window API uses `api` not `electronAPI`** — `contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld("api", ...)` verified in `preload/index.ts:90`.
5. **Window interface update is already committed** — `getMcpServers`/`setMcpServers` already added to `packages/app/src/app.tsx:83-84`.
6. **Confirmation dialog uses inline pattern** — `@opencode-ai/ui/confirm-dialog` doesn't exist. Follow `dialog-manage-agents.tsx:113-145` (inline `confirmDelete` signal + `Show` swap).
7. **Add structured error handling** — No silent `.catch(() => {})`. Every step logs and reports failures.
8. **Fix i18n namespace** — New keys use `dialog.mcp.*` prefix consistent with existing `dialog.mcp.title`/`dialog.mcp.description`.

## Current State

### What Already Exists (committed + dirty WIP)
- **`packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/sdk.gen.ts`** — `Mcp` class with `status()`, `add()`, `connect()`, `disconnect()`, `auth` sub-client with `remove()`, `start()`, `callback()`, `authenticate()`. All verified.
- **`packages/sdk/js/src/v2/gen/types.gen.ts`** — `McpLocalConfig`, `McpRemoteConfig`, `McpOAuthConfig` types.
- **`packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts`** — `RESERVED_STORE_NAMES` at module level (line 10), `isValidAgentDef`/`validateAndFilterAgents` helpers at lines 22-35. Generic `store-get`/`store-set`/`store-delete`/`store-clear`/`store-keys`/`store-length` handlers (inside `registerIpcHandlers`). Dedicated `get-desktop-custom-agents`/`set-desktop-custom-agents` handlers at lines 242-247.
- **`packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts`** — `contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld("api", api)` at line 90.
- **`packages/app/src/app.tsx:83-84`** — `getMcpServers: () => Promise<unknown[]>` and `setMcpServers: (servers: unknown[]) => Promise<void>` already added to Window interface (dirty).
- **`packages/app/src/components/dialog-select-mcp.tsx`** — 323 lines, 81% dirty. Has: `McpHealthIndicator`, server listing via `<List>`, connect/disconnect toggle via `<Switch>`, inline add form (name + URL only, hardcoded `type:"remote"`), DropdownMenu with Delete (only disconnects), `<Button>` bars for add/submit. `FormMode = "list" | "add"`.
- **`packages/app/src/components/dialog-edit-agent.tsx`** — Reference pattern for dialog-edit-mcp.tsx (195 lines, committed).

### What Must Be Added/Changed
1. **IPC handlers** — `getDesktopMcpServers`/`setDesktopMcpServers` in ipc.ts (follow custom agents pattern)
2. **Preload bridge** — Wire `getMcpServers`/`setMcpServers` in preload/index.ts
3. **Preload types** — Add `McpServerEntry` type to preload/types.ts
4. **Types file** — `packages/app/src/types/mcp.ts` (type-safe re-exports)
5. **Edit dialog** — NEW `dialog-edit-mcp.tsx` following dialog-edit-agent.tsx pattern
6. **Select dialog** — DESTRUCTIVE edits to dialog-select-mcp.tsx: replace inline add form with DialogEditMcp, fix delete, add edit
7. **i18n keys** — Add to en.ts

## Implementation Steps

### Step 1: IPC Layer — Add MCP Server Store
**Files:**
- `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts` (additive — current file is dirty with refactoring)
- `packages/desktop/src/preload/types.ts` (additive)
- `packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts` (additive)

**Current state of ipc.ts:**
```typescript
// Line 10 (module level):
const RESERVED_STORE_NAMES = ["desktop-custom-agents"]

// Lines 22-35 (module level):
function isValidAgentDef(val: unknown): val is Record<string, unknown> { ... }
function validateAndFilterAgents(raw: unknown): unknown[] { ... }

// Lines 242-247 (inside registerIpcHandlers):
ipcMain.handle("get-desktop-custom-agents", () => { ... })
ipcMain.handle("set-desktop-custom-agents", (_event, agents) => { ... })
```

**Changes:**
1. Add `"desktop-mcp-servers"` to `RESERVED_STORE_NAMES` array (line 10)
2. Add validation helpers alongside existing ones (after line 35):
```typescript
function isValidMcpEntry(val: unknown): val is Record<string, unknown> {
  if (typeof val !== "object" || val === null) return false
  const obj = val as Record<string, unknown>
  // name must be string, config must be object, type must be "local"|"remote"
  return typeof obj.name === "string" && typeof obj.config === "object" && obj.config !== null &&
    (obj.config as any).type === "local" || (obj.config as any).type === "remote"
}

function validateMcpServers(raw: unknown): unknown[] {
  if (!Array.isArray(raw)) return []
  return raw.filter((item): item is Record<string, unknown> => {
    if (isValidMcpEntry(item)) return true
    console.warn("Dropping invalid MCP server entry:", item)
    return false
  })
}
```
3. Add IPC handlers after line 247:
```typescript
ipcMain.handle("get-desktop-mcp-servers", () => {
  const store = getStore("desktop-mcp-servers")
  return validateMcpServers(store.get("servers"))
})
ipcMain.handle("set-desktop-mcp-servers", (_event, servers: unknown[]) => {
  const store = getStore("desktop-mcp-servers")
  store.set("servers", validateMcpServers(servers))
})
```
4. Add `McpServerEntry` type to preload/types.ts (after AgentDef, around line 42):
```typescript
export type McpServerEntry = {
  name: string
  config: {
    type: "local" | "remote"
    command?: string[]        // required if type=local
    url?: string              // required if type=remote
    environment?: Record<string, string>
    headers?: Record<string, string>
    oauth?: {
      clientId?: string
      clientSecret?: string
      scope?: string
      callbackPort?: number
      redirectUri?: string
    } | false
    timeout?: number
    enabled?: boolean
  }
}
```
5. Add `getMcpServers`/`setMcpServers` to `ElectronAPI` interface in preload/types.ts
6. Wire in preload/index.ts:
```typescript
getMcpServers: () => ipcRenderer.invoke("get-desktop-mcp-servers"),
setMcpServers: (servers) => ipcRenderer.invoke("set-desktop-mcp-servers", servers),
```

**Validation:** `bun typecheck` in packages/desktop

### Step 2: Window API Declaration
**File:** `packages/app/src/app.tsx`

**Current state (already dirty, lines 83-84):**
```typescript
getMcpServers?: () => Promise<unknown[]>
setMcpServers?: (servers: unknown[]) => Promise<void>
```

**Change:** Tighten types from `unknown[]` to `McpServerEntry[]` after importing the type.

**Risks:** None — just type refinement.

**Validation:** `bun typecheck` in packages/app

### Step 3: Edit/Create Dialog Component
**File:** NEW `packages/app/src/components/dialog-edit-mcp.tsx`

Follow the `dialog-edit-agent.tsx` pattern exactly. Key conventions:
- **Props:** `{ entry?: McpServerEntry; onSave: (entry: McpServerEntry) => void }`
- **Form state:** Use `createStore` from solid-js/store (like `dialog-select-mcp.tsx:3`), not individual `createSignal`s
- **Dialog title:** Use `language.t()` not raw strings (unlike existing code sketch)
- **Footer:** Cancel (ghost) + Save (primary) buttons in `flex justify-end gap-2 pt-2`
- **Save button:** `disabled={!name()}` guard

**Fields (in order):**
1. **Name** — TextField. Disabled in edit mode (MCP servers are keyed by name).
2. **Type selector** — Use `<Select>` with options "local"/"remote" (following same Select API as dialog-edit-agent.tsx line 4's import)
3. **Local fields** (shown when type=local):
   - Command — TextField (multi-valued string)
   - Environment — key-value pair rows (add/remove buttons)
4. **Remote fields** (shown when type=remote):
   - URL — TextField
   - Headers — key-value pair rows
   - OAuth — collapsible section with: clientId, clientSecret, scope, callbackPort, redirectUri
5. **Timeout** — TextField (number, ms)
6. **Enabled** — Switch (default true)

**Type precision for SDK match:**
The SDK's `McpLocalConfig` has `command: string[]` (required), `environment?: Record<string,string>`, `enabled?: boolean`, `timeout?: number`.
The SDK's `McpRemoteConfig` has `url: string` (required), `headers?: Record<string,string>`, `oauth?: McpOAuthConfig | false`, `enabled?: boolean`, `timeout?: number`.
The EditMcp component's save handler must produce types that match these SDK shapes exactly.

**Command field:** Store as string internally (`join(" ")` for display), split on whitespace for output. Note: whitespace splitting breaks for quoted args — document this limitation and defer proper shell-parsing to a follow-up.

### Step 4: Enhance dialog-select-mcp.tsx
**File:** `packages/app/src/components/dialog-select-mcp.tsx` (dirty — DESTRUCTIVE edits, not additive)

**Warning:** This file is 81% dirty (261 uncommitted lines). The following edits replace significant portions. Must preserve existing health indicators, toggle, auth trigger, and list rendering.

**Changes:**

**a) Fix delete flow** — Replace `handleRemove` with three-function pattern using inline confirmation (following dialog-manage-agents.tsx:113-145):
```typescript
const [confirmDelete, setConfirmDelete] = createSignal<string | null>(null)

const handleDeleteClick = (name: string) => setConfirmDelete(name)

const handleConfirmDelete = async () => {
  const name = confirmDelete()
  if (!name) return
  try {
    // Step 1: Log intent
    console.log("Removing MCP server:", name)
    // Step 2: Disconnect
    await sdk.client.mcp.disconnect({ name })
  } catch (err) {
    console.warn("Disconnect failed during delete (server may already be disconnected):", err)
  }
  try {
    // Step 3: Remove auth tokens
    await sdk.client.mcp.auth.remove({ name })
  } catch (err) {
    console.warn("Auth removal failed during delete:", err)
  }
  try {
    // Step 4: Remove from electron-store (always last — entry stays if earlier steps fail)
    const servers = await window.api.getMcpServers()!
    await window.api.setMcpServers!(servers.filter((s: any) => s.name !== name))
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Failed to remove MCP server from store:", err)
    // Show toast
  }
  // Step 5: Refetch
  await queryClient.refetchQueries(queryOptions.mcp(pathKey(sync.directory)))
  setConfirmDelete(null)
}

const handleCancelDelete = () => setConfirmDelete(null)
```

**b) Replace inline add form with DialogEditMcp** — Remove the `<Show when={!isFormMode()} fallback={...}>` inline add form (lines 169-193) and the inline add/submit buttons (lines 306-322). Replace with DialogEditMcp triggered by a signal:
```typescript
const [editingEntry, setEditingEntry] = createSignal<McpServerEntry | undefined>()

const handleSaveServer = async (entry: McpServerEntry) => {
  try {
    // 1. Store in electron-store
    const servers = await window.api.getMcpServers()!
    const existing = servers.findIndex((s: any) => s.name === entry.name)
    if (existing >= 0) {
      servers[existing] = entry
    } else {
      servers.push(entry)
    }
    await window.api.setMcpServers!(servers)
    
    // 2. Notify MCP service (add or reconnect)
    await sdk.client.mcp.add({ name: entry.name, config: entry.config })
    
    // 3. Refetch
    await queryClient.refetchQueries(queryOptions.mcp(pathKey(sync.directory)))
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Failed to save MCP server:", err)
  }
  setEditingEntry(undefined)
}
```

**c) Add edit button to DropdownMenu** — Insert before the Delete item:
```tsx
<DropdownMenu.Item onSelect={() => setEditingEntry({ name: i.name, config: ... })}>
  <DropdownMenu.ItemLabel>{language.t("dialog.mcp.action.edit")}</DropdownMenu.ItemLabel>
</DropdownMenu.Item>
```

**d) Replace inline delete DropdownMenu.Item with inline confirmation** — In the list item rendering, when `confirmDelete() === i.name`, swap the DropdownMenu for inline confirm buttons (matching dialog-manage-agents.tsx pattern).

**e) Add startup sync** — Not a `createEffect` (which fires on dependency changes), use an imperative `loaded()` guard pattern:
```typescript
let syncedStartup = false

// Called after sync.data.mcp first becomes available
const syncMcpWithStore = async () => {
  if (syncedStartup) return
  syncedStartup = true
  try {
    const storedServers = await window.api.getMcpServers()!
    const existingNames = new Set(Object.keys(sync.data.mcp ?? {}))
    for (const entry of storedServers) {
      if (!existingNames.has(entry.name)) {
        try {
          await sdk.client.mcp.add({ name: entry.name, config: entry.config })
        } catch (err) {
          console.warn("Failed to restore MCP server on startup:", entry.name, err)
        }
      }
    }
  } catch (err) {
    console.error("Failed to sync MCP servers from electron-store:", err)
  }
}
```

**f) Change FormMode:** Keep as `"list"` only for the mode toggle. Add/edit flows go through DialogEditMcp overlay.

Reuse existing `"common.cancel"` and `"common.save"` keys (already present).

### Step 6: Type declarations
**File:** NEW `packages/app/src/types/mcp.ts`

```typescript
import type {
  McpLocalConfig,
  McpRemoteConfig,
  McpOAuthConfig as SdkMcpOAuthConfig,
} from "@opencode-ai/sdk/v2/client"

export type { McpLocalConfig, McpRemoteConfig }
export type McpOAuthConfig = SdkMcpOAuthConfig
export type McpServerConfig = McpLocalConfig | McpRemoteConfig
export type McpServerEntry = {
  name: string
  config: McpServerConfig
}
```

Note: The discriminated union `McpServerConfig = McpLocalConfig | McpRemoteConfig` ensures:
- `type: "local"` servers require `command: string[]`
- `type: "remote"` servers require `url: string`
TypeScript will enforce this at compile time.

## Dependency Graph
```
Step 1 (IPC layer) ──→ Step 2 (Window types) ──→ Step 3 (dialog-edit-mcp)
       │                                              ↓
       └──→ Step 6 (types/mcp.ts) ──────────→ Step 4 (dialog-select-mcp)
                                                      ↑
                                              Step 5 (i18n)
```
Steps 1, 5, 6 can be parallelized. Step 3 depends on Step 1+2. Step 4 depends on Steps 3 and 6.

## Files Changed Summary

| File | Change Type | Description |
|------|-------------|-------------|
| `packages/desktop/src/main/ipc.ts` | Additive | Add "desktop-mcp-servers" to RESERVED_STORE_NAMES, add validation helpers + IPC handlers |
| `packages/desktop/src/preload/types.ts` | Additive | Add McpServerEntry type, getMcpServers/setMcpServers to ElectronAPI |
| `packages/desktop/src/preload/index.ts` | Additive | Wire getMcpServers/setMcpServers through IPC |
| `packages/app/src/app.tsx` | Type refine | Tighten getMcpServers/setMcpServers types from unknown[] to McpServerEntry[] |
| `packages/app/src/types/mcp.ts` | NEW | SDK type re-exports with discriminated union |
| `packages/app/src/components/dialog-edit-mcp.tsx` | NEW | Edit/create dialog component (200-250 lines) |
| `packages/app/src/components/dialog-select-mcp.tsx` | Destructive | Replace add form with DialogEditMcp, fix delete (inline confirm), add edit button, add startup sync |
| `packages/app/src/i18n/en.ts` | Additive | New i18n keys for form fields, actions, confirmation |

## Risks & Mitigations (Updated)

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|------|----------|------------|
| dialog-select-mcp.tsx is 81% dirty — destructive edits overlap with existing uncommitted WIP | High | Read the current dirty state before editing. Make changes in targeted search_replace blocks. Never write_file on this file. |
| Startup sync racing with initial server sync query | Medium | Use imperative `syncedStartup` flag guard, not `createEffect`. Sync after first successful sync.data.mcp population. |
| Silent failures on delete/store operations | Medium (was High, now addressed) | Structured error handling with try/catch per step, console logging, toast on critical failures. No `.catch(() => {})`. electron-store removal is the final step so config survives auth/disconnect failures. |
| electron-store dual-source drift | Medium | Electron-store takes precedence for desktop-managed servers. Display a note if electron-store entry differs from config file entry. |
| OAuth secrets in electron-store (plaintext) | Low | Follow existing pattern (custom agents also store prompts/metadata). safeStorage encryption deferred to follow-up. |
| Type mismatch between SDK generated types and Effect Schema | Low | Import from SDK generated types (types.gen.ts) which is the single source of truth for the REST API shape. |
| Command string splitting breaks for quoted args | Low | Document as known limitation. Use `split(/\s+/)` for initial release. File follow-up for proper shell parsing. |

## Rollback Plan (Revised)

Because the dialog-select-mcp.tsx edits are **destructive** and interleaved with dirty WIP, rollback requires:
1. `git checkout -- packages/app/src/components/dialog-select-mcp.tsx` (loses ALL dirty WIP — coordinate with owning session)
2. Delete `packages/app/src/components/dialog-edit-mcp.tsx`
3. Delete `packages/app/src/types/mcp.ts`
4. Revert ipc.ts, preload/types.ts, preload/index.ts (search_replace for each addition)
5. Revert app.tsx type changes
6. Revert i18n/en.ts additions

## Follow-up Issues

1. **OAuth secret encryption:** `safeStorage.encryptString()` should be used for McpServerEntry.config.oauth.clientSecret. Currently plaintext in electron-store.
2. **Proper shell-parsing for MCP commands:** The whitespace-splitting approach breaks for commands with quoted arguments (`/bin/sh -c "echo hi"`). Consider `shell-quote` npm package for round-trip reliability.
3. **Electron-store / config file reconciliation:** Servers added via CLI won't appear in desktop UI and vice versa. Eventually need a strategy to merge sources or designate one as canonical.
revision_notes: Revision 2 incorporates all 3 blocking findings from the critic review. Fixes: (1) ipc.ts references updated to reflect module-level helpers, (2) SDK API surface now verified with specific line references to sdk.gen.ts, (3) dialog-select-mcp.tsx acknowledged as destructive edits, (4) window.api not window.electronAPI, (5) app.tsx Window update already committed, (6) inline confirmation pattern (no ConfirmDialog), (7) structured error handling throughout delete/sync flows, (8) createStore not createSignal, (9) language.t() not raw strings, (10) McpServerEntry type uses SDK's discriminated union (McpConfigLocal | McpConfigRemote).
